name: UserInfo endpoint
outcome: A relying party can present an access token to a UserInfo endpoint and receive the end-user's scope-derived claims, proven end-to-end by the keystone test using an independent JWT verifier (the `jose` library) — not the library's own JwtService.
scope: Add the UserInfo handler and the AuthorizationServer.userInfo() method, both delegating access-token verification to the AccessTokenVerifier seam from issue 2. The keystone e2e exercises the full OIDC loop with an independent verifier and asserts header content explicitly. Adds a docs page section for UserInfo as part of this issue.
acceptance_criteria: UserInfo returns { sub, ...scope-derived claims } as JSON for a valid openid-scoped access token. Content-Type: application/json. Cache-Control: no-store. (Spec-required headers, not optional)

sub is applied LAST in the response object — consumer getUserClaims cannot overwrite sub via spread order; strip sub from the filtered claims before re-adding it from the shared canonical stringify helper
UserInfo sub equals the ID token sub byte-for-byte (asserted in the keystone test by decoding both)

Access-token verification goes through the AccessTokenVerifier seam (issue 2). UserInfo does NOT re-implement signature / typ / iss / aud checks — it calls the seam and translates seam errors into UserInfo HTTP responses
A token without the openid scope yields 403 insufficient_scope with WWW-Authenticate: Bearer error="insufficient_scope", error_description="openid scope required", scope="openid"

An invalid or expired token yields 401 invalid_token with WWW-Authenticate: Bearer error="invalid_token", error_description="<specific reason>". The error_description is pinned in tests, not described as 'clear'
A token whose JOSE header typ is not at+jwt — e.g. an id_token presented as a bearer token — yields 401 invalid_token via the seam's typ check
A token whose iss does not equal the configured issuer yields 401 invalid_token via the seam's iss check (closes the token-substitution gap)
A revoked access token (when getByAccessToken is implemented and indicates revocation) yields 401 invalid_token

Keystone e2e on the vanilla adapter: authorize with scope=openid + nonce + PKCE; exchange the code; verify the id_token using the `jose` library (NOT JwtService — self-referential verification cannot catch a JWK-export bug) against the live /jwks endpoint; call /userinfo; assert decoded id_token.sub === userinfo sub; assert Content-Type and Cache-Control headers. Timer strategy: vi.useRealTimers() inside the describe block — test/setup.ts installs fake timers globally and a real-clock JWKS verify cannot work under frozen time
steps: src/oidc — add a UserInfo handler that extracts the bearer access token (Authorization header and form/query), delegates verification to the AccessTokenVerifier seam, and handles the seam's typed errors
UserInfo handler — translate seam errors to HTTP: invalid_token → 401 with the pinned WWW-Authenticate; if the token lacks the openid scope → 403 insufficient_scope with the pinned WWW-Authenticate

UserInfo handler — call getUserClaims with the user identifier produced by the shared canonical stringify helper (the same one used by issue 4's ID token builder); apply the issue 6 scope filter; strip any sub from the filtered output; return { ...claims, sub } as JSON with sub from the same helper, Content-Type: application/json, Cache-Control: no-store
UserInfo handler — when the token repository implements getByAccessToken, check revocation and treat revoked tokens as invalid_token
AuthorizationServer — add a userInfo(req) method delegating to the handler

test/unit — sub-last guard (consumer claims with sub spread first do not win); typ rejection (id_token as bearer); iss rejection (foreign issuer); revoked-token rejection; missing-openid-scope 403; expired-token 401; the exact WWW-Authenticate header values

test/e2e — keystone test on the vanilla adapter as specified in the acceptance criteria. The test uses `jose` (a dev dependency) for independent verification. The package.json adds `jose` to devDependencies
docs site — add a UserInfo endpoint reference page as part of this issue, not deferred
notes: Routing access-token verification through the AccessTokenVerifier seam (issue 2) closes MF-3 and MF-4 in one place: the seam owns the algorithm pin, typ:at+jwt check, iss equality, and the recorded v1 aud policy, regardless of which JwtInterface the consumer supplies. The seam is also unit-testable independently so consumer-side resource-server adapters can reuse it. getUserClaims is keyed by userId, not a loaded user object, so no user repository is needed in the OIDC config. UserInfo returns plain JSON only in v1 (signed/encrypted UserInfo deferred per PRD out_of_scope). The keystone e2e is the highest-value test — it proves the cryptographic plumbing with an independent verifier.
